seoTitle: Mount Ney vs Mount Palmer — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Ney and Mount Palmer. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Ney vs Mount Palmer: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Ney and Mount Palmer.
heading: Mount Ney vs Mount Palmer — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Ney, Western Australia and Mount Palmer, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Ney has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Palmer (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Ney or Mount Palmer safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Ney has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Mount Palmer scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Ney scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Palmer scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Ney or Mount Palmer?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Ney has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
